questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的盈利水平和现金流情况怎么样？
questions.answer:
2024年，路特斯科技的盈利水平和现金流情况呈现出显著的挑战与进展并存的特点。在盈利能力方面，公司在上半年展现了强劲的增长势头，交付量同比大幅增长239%，达到4,873辆。尽管第一季度净亏损为2.58亿美元，但营收和毛利率均显著提升。第一季度营收达到1.73亿美元，同比增长811%，毛利率从2023年同期的1%提升至18%。此外，公司推出的定制化服务和技术相关收入预计将为未来利润率做出积极贡献。

在现金流方面，路特斯科技仍面临一定的压力。2024年上半年，公司经营活动现金流为负4.68361亿美元，较2023年同期的负3.037亿美元有所增加，表明运营资金需求较大。然而，通过融资活动，公司获得了12.488亿美元的现金流入，主要得益于普通股发行和银行贷款的增加。这使得上半年现金及受限现金净增加了2.1668亿美元，显示出公司在资金管理上取得了一定成效。

为改善财务状况，路特斯科技启动了“Win26”计划，目标是在2026年实现正的经营现金流和EBITDA。这一战略表明公司正在积极采取措施，为未来的持续发展奠定基础。总体来看，尽管面临短期挑战，路特斯科技在2024年展现了显著的财务增长和市场扩展能力，未来发展前景值得期待。

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. The company incurred a net loss of US\$460.3 million, US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$468.4 million, US\$386.9 million, US\$351.4 million, and US\$126.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. As of June 30, 2024, and as of December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company's accumulated deficit was US\$2.0 billion, US\$1.6 billion, US\$846.8 million, and US\$122.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$49.5 million, US\$213.7 million, US\$133.3 million, and US\$34.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. The company will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations.

metadata.title_summary:

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has not been profitable since its inception. The company incurred a net loss of US\$460.3 million, US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$468.4 million, US\$386.9 million, US\$351.4 million, and US\$126.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. As of June 30, 2024, and as of December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company's accumulated deficit was US\$2.0 billion, US\$1.6 billion, US\$846.8 million, and US\$122.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$49.5 million, US\$213.7 million, US\$133.3 million, and US\$34.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. The company will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We have not been profitable and had negative net cash flows from operations. If we do not effectively manage our cash and other liquid financial assets, execute our plan to increase profitability and obtain additional financing, we may not be able to continue as a going concern. 
summary: The company has faced significant financial challenges since its inception, reporting substantial net losses totaling $460.3 million, $750.3 million, $724.6 million, and $110.5 million for the six months ended June 30 in 2024, 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Alongside these losses, the company has experienced negative net cash flows from operating activities of $468.4 million, $386.9 million, $351.4 million, and $126.5 million for the same periods. As of June 30, 2024, the accumulated deficit reached $2.0 billion, a significant increase from $1.6 billion in December 2023, $846.8 million in 2022, and $122.8 million in 2021. Capital expenditures have also been substantial, amounting to $49.5 million, $213.7 million, $133.3 million, and $34.6 million for the respective six-month periods.

To finance its operations, the company has primarily depended on proceeds from exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings. However, it requires additional liquidity over the next 12 months and is actively exploring various funding strategies, including loans and enhancing operational efficiency. The feasibility of these plans remains uncertain and is influenced by factors outside the company's control.

The company is under pressure to generate positive cash flow due to existing contractual obligations and anticipates ongoing investments in vehicle production, sales network expansion, and research and development (R&D). However, these investments may not result in timely revenue increases or positive cash flow. If the company fails to continue as a going concern, it may encounter difficulties in securing financing on acceptable terms, which raises substantial doubt about its operational viability. Furthermore, the financial statements do not account for potential adjustments should the company be unable to maintain normal operations, potentially leading to significant losses for investors. The company also faces risks such as lack of demand, increasing competition, and unforeseen expenses, all of which may impede its ability to generate revenue and achieve profitability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year?
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology demonstrated exceptional growth, reporting total revenue of $398 million, which reflects a remarkable year-on-year increase of 206%. The company's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained robust, exceeding $100,000, and significantly outpacing growth in both the automotive industry and the luxury car segment. This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Lotus achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, marking a staggering 239% increase year-on-year, thereby establishing itself as the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car manufacturers. Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The company has also embarked on strategic collaborations with leading automotive brands, which are expected to enhance technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and technology development services. The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1**: For 2023, Lotus Technology's projected revenues are approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion. This figure is expected to grow significantly to about $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increase to a range of $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion in 2025.
- **Row 2**: The Gross Profit for 2023 is estimated to range between $0.3 billion and $0.4 billion. It is forecasted to rise to $1.1 billion to $1.3 billion in 2024, and then grow further to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion in 2025.
- **Row 3**: The projected Gross Margin, as a percentage, is approximately 14.0% to 16.0% in 2023. This margin is expected to improve to around 18.0% to 20.0% in 2024 and further increase to approximately 21.0% to 23.0% in 2025.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial forecasts covering revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates for 2023 to 2025, which were prepared without adherence to SEC guidelines or U.S. GAAP. These projections were intended for internal use to assist LCAA's due diligence prior to the Business Combination approval. Subsequent updates were shared in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." 

Lotus Tech emphasizes that these projections are subjective and based on various assumptions, many of which are uncertain and may change over time, potentially leading to significant discrepancies between projected and actual results. The company does not publicly disclose such forecasts and does not intend to do so in the future, highlighting that the projections should not be relied upon as guarantees of future performance.

The Financial Projections indicate a growth trajectory, with revenues expected to rise from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion in 2023, reaching between $6.2 billion and $6.6 billion in 2024, and further increasing to between $8.5 billion and $8.9 billion in 2025. Gross profit is projected to grow from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to between $1.8 billion and $2.0 billion by 2025, with gross margins improving from 14%–16% to 21%–23% over the same period.

Lotus Tech anticipates vehicle sales will exceed 76,000 units by 2025, driven by models such as the Eletre and Type 133, with significant contributions from both vehicle-related and technology-related revenues. The company also projects growth in ecosystem-related revenue through charging services and peripheral product sales. However, stakeholders are cautioned about the inherent risks and uncertainties that could impact these forecasts, including market conditions, competitive pressures, and operational challenges.

Overall, while Lotus Tech's management has outlined optimistic projections based on reasonable assumptions, the company acknowledges the potential for actual results to vary significantly from these estimates, underscoring the need for caution among investors and stakeholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Certain Prospective Operational and Financial Information 
summary: On November 28, 2022, Lotus Tech provided LCAA with initial financial projections for 2023 to 2025, including revenue, gross profit, and EBITDA margin estimates, to assist in their due diligence process prior to the approval of a business combination. These projections were updated in June and October 2023, collectively referred to as the "Financial Projections." It is important to note that these projections were not prepared in accordance with SEC guidelines or GAAP and were intended for internal use, reflecting management's expectations and assumptions about future performance, which may be subject to significant uncertainties.

The Financial Projections indicate a strong growth trajectory, with projected revenues for 2023 ranging from approximately $2.2 billion to $2.5 billion, increasing to $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion in 2024, and reaching $8.5 billion to $8.9 billion by 2025. Gross profit is expected to rise from $0.3 billion to $0.4 billion in 2023 to $1.8 billion to $2.0 billion by 2025, while gross margins are projected to improve from 14.0%–16.0% in 2023 to 21.0%–23.0% in 2025.

Lotus Tech anticipates selling over 76,000 vehicles by 2025, driven by several key models, including the Eletre and Type 133. The company expects significant contributions from vehicle sales, technology-related revenue, and ecosystem services. However, the projections carry inherent risks, including potential delays in vehicle launches and market dynamics, which could lead to discrepancies between projected and actual results.

Lotus Tech emphasizes that the Financial Projections should not be relied upon as definitive guidance, as they are speculative and subject to numerous uncertainties. The company does not guarantee the accuracy of these forecasts and will not reference them in future reports post-business combination. The projections were prepared by management and have not been audited by KPMG Huazhen LLP, which only provided assurance on historical financial information. Overall, while the projections suggest a promising outlook for Lotus Tech, stakeholders are advised to approach them with caution due to the various risks and assumptions involved.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Table Summary:** This table presents the financial performance of Lotus Technology Inc. for the first half (first half) and second quarter (2Q) of fiscal year 2024 (FY2024) compared to corresponding periods in FY2023. Metrics include deliveries, revenues, cost of revenues, gross profit, gross profit margin, operating loss, net loss, adjusted net loss, and adjusted EBITDA, with year-over-year (YoY) percentage changes provided.  
- **Context:** The table details unaudited financial results to analyze performance trends over time, emphasizing significant year-over-year growth and challenges across various metrics.  
- **Special Notes:** All values are in millions of US dollars, unless otherwise noted. Unit measurements like deliveries are in units, and percentages for margins and YoY changes are explicitly listed. Footnotes 1 and 2 reference specific calculations or adjustments.  

---

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1:** Deliveries in 1H2024 reached 4,873 units, compared to 1,439 units in 1H2023, reflecting an impressive 239% YoY increase. In 2Q2024, deliveries totaled 2,679 units, up 128% from 1,175 units in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 2:** Revenues grew significantly to $398 million in 1H2024 from $130 million in 1H2023, representing a 206% YoY increase. Revenues in 2Q2024 amounted to $225 million, a 103% increase from $111 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 3:** The cost of revenues increased to $347 million in 1H2024 from $124 million in 1H2023, a 180% YoY rise. In 2Q2024, the cost of revenues was $204 million, showing a 95% increase compared to $105 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 4:** Gross profit in 1H2024 improved to $51 million, up substantially from $6 million in 1H2023, marking a 731% YoY growth. In 2Q2024, gross profit was $21 million, a 246% increase from $6 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 5:** The gross profit margin for 1H2024 stood at 13%, significantly higher than the 5% recorded in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the margin reached 9%, compared to 5% in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 6:** Operating loss expanded to $438 million in 1H2024, a 27% increase from $344 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, the operating loss was $204 million, compared to $183 million in 2Q2023, marking a 12% YoY growth.  
- **Row 7:** Net loss for 1H2024 reached $460 million, a 30% increase from $353 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, net loss stood at $202 million, 4% higher than $193 million in 2Q2023.  
- **Row 8:** Adjusted net loss in 1H2024 was $424 million, rising 20% from $353 million in 1H2023. For 2Q2024, adjusted net loss was $201 million, compared to $193 million in 2Q2023, a 4% increase.  
- **Row 9:** Adjusted EBITDA in 1H2024 stood at a loss of $382 million, up 15% from $333 million in 1H2023. In 2Q2024, it was a loss of $177 million, almost unchanged (a 1% decrease) from $178 million in 2Q2023.

- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

- **Row 5:** No put option liabilities for related parties were reported as of June 30, 2024, or December 31, 2023.  
- **Row 6:** Warrant liabilities were introduced in the June 30, 2024 period, totaling **$5,549**, with no comparable value in December 2023.  
- **Row 7:** Exchangeable notes rose slightly to **$77,087** as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$75,678** in December 2023.  
- **Row 8:** Convertible notes with third parties declined from **$81,635** in December 2023 to **$75,970** as of June 30, 2024.  
- **Row 9:** Deferred income amounted to **$296,489** as of June 30, 2024, an increase from **$270,097** in December 2023.  
- **Row 10:** Other non-current liabilities with third parties increased to **$108,293** as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$103,403** at the end of 2023.  
- **Row 11:** Other non-current liabilities with related parties declined marginally to **$1,548** as of June 30, 2024, from **$1,634** in December 2023.  
- **Row 12:** Total non-current liabilities grew to **$839,881** as of June 30, 2024, up from **$654,569** in December 2023.  

### **Liabilities:**
- **Row 13:** The total liabilities increased significantly to **$2,680,335** as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$2,411,850** at year-end 2023.  

### **Mezzanine Equity:**  
- **Row 14:** As of June 30, 2024, there is no balance for Series Pre-A Redeemable Convertible Preferred Shares or Series A Redeemable Convertible Preferred Shares, whereas amounts of **$184,509** and **$199,021**, respectively, were recorded as of December 31, 2023.  
- **Row 15:** Total mezzanine equity was nil as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$383,530** as of December 31, 2023.  

### **Shareholders' Deficit:**  
- **Row 16:** Ordinary shares decreased to **7** as of June 30, 2024, from **21** in December 2023.  
- **Row 17:** Additional paid-in capital amounted to **$1,855,063** as of June 30, 2024, significantly increasing from **$358,187** in December 2023.  
- **Row 18:** Accumulated other comprehensive income showed a slight increase to **$25,278** as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$25,267** in December 2023.  
- **Row 19:** Accumulated deficit deepened further, reaching **$(2,048,092)** as of June 30, 2024, compared to **$(1,588,774)** in December 2023.  
- **Row 20:** Total shareholders’ deficit attributable to ordinary shareholders improved to **$(167,744)** as of June 30, 2024, in contrast to **$(1,205,297)** recorded in December 2023.  
- **Row 21:** Non-controlling interests were reduced to **$(6,337)** as of June 30, 2024, from **$(5,404)** in December 2023.  
- **Row 22:** Total shareholders’ deficit declined to **$(174,081)** as of June 30, 2024, compared to a larger deficit of **$(1,210,701)** recorded in December 2023.  

### **Overall Total:**  
- **Row 23:** The combined total of liabilities, mezzanine equity, and shareholders’ deficit increased to **$2,506,254** as of June 30, 2024, from **$1,584,679** as of December 31, 2023.

Row 23: Total comprehensive loss for the period in June 30, 2024, was reported at $459,308 thousand, compared to $314,345 thousand for the same period last year.

Row 24: Weighted average number of ordinary shares outstanding used in computing net loss per ordinary share, basic and diluted, was 616,941,673 in 2024, a significant rise from 474,612,619 shares in 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 7,600 vehicles were delivered in the first nine months of 2024, a year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue reached $653 million, more than doubling year-on-year. Revenue from the intelligent driving business generated from customers other than Lotus surged to $11 million, with a year-on-year growth of 450%. Lotus Technology launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology that provides high performance with a combined range of over 1,100 kilometers. New York – Nov 21, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and first nine months ended September 30, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Third
Quarter and First Nine Months 2024 Financial Results 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has released its unaudited financial results for the third quarter and the first nine months of 2024, concluding on September 30, 2024. During this period, the company achieved a remarkable milestone by delivering over 7,600 vehicles, which represents a substantial year-on-year increase of 136%. Total revenue for the company reached $653 million, more than doubling compared to the same period last year. 

A significant highlight of the financial results was the performance of the intelligent driving business, which generated $11 million in revenue from customers other than Lotus, showcasing an impressive year-on-year growth of 450%. 

In addition to these financial achievements, Lotus Technology announced the launch of its Hyper Hybrid EV technology on November 21, 2024. This innovative technology promises high performance with a combined driving range exceeding 1,100 kilometers, further solidifying the company's commitment to advancing electric vehicle technology.

Overall, Lotus Technology's third-quarter and nine-month results reflect strong growth and innovation within the company, positioning it favorably in the competitive automotive market.

[Table Level]  
- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand with Racing Heritage  
- **Table Summary:** This table represents a chronological history of significant milestones and achievements by Lotus in motorsports and automotive design. Key events include early wins in Le Mans, Formula 1, and Indy 500, as well as recent industry recognitions and awards. The table highlights both competitive and design excellence over multiple decades.  
- **Context:** Lotus positions itself as a legacy brand with a rich automotive and racing history, as evidenced by numerous motorsport championships and innovative products.  
- **Special Notes:** Achievements are tied to specific years and vehicle types. The vehicle names (such as Type 18 and Lotus Evija) and awards (e.g., FIA World Champions, Luxury Briefing Awards) are integral to understanding the brand's legacy.  

[Row Level]  
- **Row 1 (1957):** The Lotus Eleven achieved its first victory at Le Mans in the 750cc Index Class.  
- **Row 2 (1960):** The Lotus Type 18 secured its first Grand Prix win at Monte Carlo.  
- **Row 3 (1963):** The Lotus Type 25 earned Lotus its first Formula 1 Constructors' Championship as well as the first Formula 1 Drivers' Championship.  
- **Row 4 (1965):** The Lotus Type 38 marked Lotus's first Indy 500 victory.  
- **Row 5 (1978):** The Lotus Sunbeam celebrated its first World Rally Constructors' Championship.  
- **Row 6 (2019):** Lotus was awarded the title "Luxury brand of the year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards.  
- **Row 7 (2020):** The Lotus Evija won the 2020 MUSE Global Design Awards.  
- **Row 8 (2023):** The Lotus Eletre was recognized as the 2023 GQ SUV of the Year.  
- **Row 9 (2024):** Carwow named Lotus the "Brand of the Year," bolstering its automotive excellence reputation.  

[Achievements Summary]  
- **FIA Formula 1 Constructors’ World Champions:** 7 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Drivers’ World Championships:** 6 titles.  
- **FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins:** 81 victories.  
- **Le Mans Wins (In Class):** 9 victories.  
- **Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win:** 1 victory.  
- **FIA World Rally Championships:** 1 title.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

Despite these advancements, Lotus Technology faces challenges reflected in its financial statements, including a net loss of $258 million in Q1 2024, which marks a significant increase from the previous year. The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

- **Table Summary:** The table highlights the sales volume (in units), revenue (in $ millions), and gross profit margin (in percentages) for Lotus Technology Inc. over three periods: the 2018-2022 average, 2023 actuals, and 2024 estimates. It contrasts the historical performance of Lotus UK with projections for Lotus Technology Inc.’s anticipated growth.
- **Context:** The data underscores Lotus Technology Inc.’s significant growth trajectory, particularly in the years following 2022, as evidenced by sharp increases in sales, revenue, and profit margins. The values were converted into USD using an exchange rate of 1 British Pound = 1.2654 USD.
- **Special Notes:**  
  1. "2018-2022 Average" refers to annual averages calculated over the period between March 31, 2018, and December 31, 2022.  
  2. Revenue conversion uses an exchange rate of 1 GBP = 1.2654 USD.
  3. Gross profit margin percentages represent profitability as a portion of revenue.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1: Sales Volume (Units)**  
  In the 2018-2022 period, Lotus UK sold an average of approximately 1,200 units per year. By 2023, this figure increased significantly to 6,970 units under Lotus Technology Inc. The projection for 2024 (2024E) reflects a substantial rise to 26,000 units, which represents a dramatic scaling of operations.

- **Row 2: Revenue ($ Million)**  
  Average annual revenue during 2018-2022 for Lotus UK was approximately $100 million. This figure grew to $679 million in 2023. The revenue forecast for 2024 is projected at between $2,500 and $2,700 million (or $2.5 billion to $2.7 billion), marking a substantial financial expansion.

- **Row 3: Gross Profit Margin (%)**  
  The gross profit margin for Lotus UK during the 2018-2022 average was only 3%. By 2023, this improved to 15% under Lotus Technology Inc. The 2024 estimate predicts further growth, ranging between 17% and 19%, indicative of increased operational efficiency and profitability.  

- **Footnotes:**  
  - ¹: Data represents annual averages for the specified period, from March 31, 2018, to December 31, 2022.  
  - ²: Revenue values for 2018-2022 are approximate, rounded to the nearest 100 units or $100 million.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

title: 23. REVENUES 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Revenues for Lotus Technology Inc.**

For the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenues of $398,115, marking a substantial increase from $130,035 during the same period in 2023. This growth is attributed to significant advancements in both product sales and service offerings, categorized into sales to third parties and related parties.

**Sales to Third Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Revenue surged to $262,849 in 2024, up from $72,790 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Sales rose to $102,618 in 2024, compared to $49,146 in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $15,096 in 2024 from $2,111 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Third Parties:** Totaled $380,563 in 2024, a significant rise from $124,047 in 2023.

**Sales to Related Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Generated $535 in 2024, slightly up from $436 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Recorded sales of $91 in 2024, with no sales in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $1,704 in 2024 from $371 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Related Parties:** Reached $2,330 in 2024, compared to $807 in 2023.

**Combined Sales Subtotal:** The total revenue from goods sold to both third and related parties was $382,893 in 2024, a notable increase from $124,854 in 2023.

**Services Provided:**
- **R&D Services to Third Parties:** Generated $56 in 2024, with no revenue in 2023.
- **Other Services to Third Parties:** Valued at $4,504 in 2024, compared to $160 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Third Parties:** Totaled $4,560 in 2024, up from $160 in 2023.
- **R&D Services to Related Parties:** Increased to $6,366 in 2024 from $4,983 in 2023.
- **Other Services to Related Parties:** Rose to $4,296 in 2024 from $38 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Related Parties:** Reached $10,662 in 2024, compared to $5,021 in 2023.

**Total Services Subtotal:** The combined revenue from services provided to both third and related parties was $15,222 in 2024, up from $5,181 in 2023.

This comprehensive revenue analysis underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust growth across various product and service lines, reflecting its expanding market presence and improved operational performance. All figures are presented in thousands of US dollars, with footnotes clarifying additional service categories.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech in the first quarter of 2024 (Q1 2024) reveal significant growth across various metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (Q1 2023). The company saw a remarkable increase in vehicle deliveries, with lifestyle vehicle deliveries soaring to 1,047 units—up 344% from 236 units in Q1 2023—and sportscar deliveries reaching 1,147 units, a substantial rise from just 28 units in the previous year. Overall, combined deliveries for both categories totaled 2,194 units, reflecting an impressive 731% year-over-year growth from 264 units in Q1 2023.

Financially, Lotus Tech reported a revenue of $173 million for Q1 2024, representing an 811% increase from $19 million in Q1 2023. However, the cost of revenues also increased significantly to $143 million, leading to a gross profit of $30 million and a gross profit margin of 18%, up from 1% in the prior year. Despite these gains, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of $233 million and a net loss of $258 million, both of which widened compared to the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2024 was negative $204 million, indicating a decline in profitability.

These results underscore Lotus Tech's strategic expansion into luxury markets and its commitment to innovation under the Vision80 plan, while also highlighting the ongoing challenges in achieving profitability amidst rapid growth and investment in global market engagement.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 2024-AYEAROFGROWTH 
summary: Lotus Cars is undergoing a strategic transformation to become an intelligent luxury mobility provider, emphasizing the development and integration of Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) models. This shift aims to enhance sales volume and profit margins while positioning the company as a leader in the luxury automotive market. By focusing on advanced technologies and sustainable practices, Lotus is set to attract a growing customer base interested in eco-friendly luxury options, which is expected to drive significant growth and profitability.

In 2024, the company plans to triple its delivery volume and strengthen client relationships by introducing four new delivery models. Key initiatives will include optimizing operational efficiency, reducing costs, and implementing best practices to streamline operations. Additionally, targeted marketing, improved customer service, and a refined product assortment will be prioritized to maximize revenue per location, with continuous assessment and adaptation being crucial to achieving these goals.

Performance metrics from 2018 to 2024 indicate a remarkable growth trajectory for Lotus Cars. Sales volume increased from an average of 1,200 units annually (2018-2022) to 6,970 units in 2023, with projections for 2024 reaching 26,000 units. Revenue followed a similar trend, jumping from an average of $100 million during 2018-2022 to $679 million in 2023, with estimates for 2024 ranging between $2,500 million and $2,700 million. The gross profit margin also improved significantly, rising from an average of 3% (2018-2022) to 15% in 2023, with projections for 2024 suggesting further growth to between 17% and 19%. 

Overall, these metrics highlight Lotus Cars' ambitious trajectory towards becoming a leader in the luxury electric vehicle market, with a strong focus on growth in production, revenue, and profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Non-GAAP Financial Measures 
summary: In the evaluation of our operating results, we focus on key non-GAAP financial measures, specifically adjusted net loss and adjusted EBITDA. These measures are crucial for management's assessment of operating performance and business planning, as they help identify underlying trends that may be obscured by certain expenses included in net loss. Adjusted net loss excludes share-based compensation expenses, which do not impact income tax, while adjusted EBITDA further excludes interest income, interest expenses, income tax expenses, depreciation, and share-based compensation.

For the six months ended June 30, 2024, the company reported a net loss of $(460,252) thousand, an increase from $(352,942) thousand in the same period of 2023. The adjusted net loss for this period was $(424,358) thousand, compared to $(352,942) thousand in 2023. Notably, share-based compensation expenses amounted to $35,894 thousand for the first half of 2024, with no expenses recorded in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was $(381,667) thousand, compared to $(332,697) thousand in 2023.

Revenue for the same period totaled $398,115 thousand, representing a substantial increase of $268,080 thousand or 206.2% from $130,035 thousand in 2023. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models and sports cars. Sales of goods revenue rose to $382,893 thousand, an increase of 206.7%, while service revenues increased to $15,222 thousand, reflecting a rise of 193.8%.

The cost of revenues for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was $347,203 thousand, marking a 180.2% increase from $123,908 thousand in 2023, largely due to higher vehicle sales. Consequently, gross profit for this period was $50,912 thousand, up by $44,785 thousand or 730.9%, resulting in a gross margin of 12.8%, compared to 4.7% in 2023. This improvement was attributed to cost reductions per vehicle, increased average selling prices, and higher-margin service income.

Overall, the financial metrics indicate significant growth in revenue and gross profit, while the adjusted measures provide valuable insights into underlying operational performance. This comprehensive analysis facilitates a clearer assessment for investors, highlighting the company's robust growth trajectory despite the increase in net loss and adjusted net loss.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

In addition, Lotus Technology had negative net cash flows from operating activities of US\$468.4 million, US\$386.9 million, US\$351.4 million, and US\$126.5 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. As of June 30, 2024, and as of December 31, 2023, 2022, and 2021, the company's accumulated deficit was US\$2.0 billion, US\$1.6 billion, US\$846.8 million, and US\$122.8 million, respectively. Lotus Technology incurred capital expenditures of US\$49.5 million, US\$213.7 million, US\$133.3 million, and US\$34.6 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024, and in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Historically, Lotus Technology relied principally on proceeds from the issuance of exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings to finance its operations and business expansion. The company will require additional liquidity to continue its operations over the next 12 months. Lotus Technology is evaluating strategies to obtain the required additional funding for future operations.

title: We have not been profitable and had negative net cash flows from operations. If we do not effectively manage our cash and other liquid financial assets, execute our plan to increase profitability and obtain additional financing, we may not be able to continue as a going concern. 
summary: The company has faced significant financial challenges since its inception, reporting substantial net losses totaling $460.3 million, $750.3 million, $724.6 million, and $110.5 million for the six months ended June 30 in 2024, 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively. Alongside these losses, the company has experienced negative net cash flows from operating activities of $468.4 million, $386.9 million, $351.4 million, and $126.5 million for the same periods. As of June 30, 2024, the accumulated deficit reached $2.0 billion, a significant increase from $1.6 billion in December 2023, $846.8 million in 2022, and $122.8 million in 2021. Capital expenditures have also been substantial, amounting to $49.5 million, $213.7 million, $133.3 million, and $34.6 million for the respective six-month periods.

To finance its operations, the company has primarily depended on proceeds from exchangeable notes, convertible notes, and related party borrowings. However, it requires additional liquidity over the next 12 months and is actively exploring various funding strategies, including loans and enhancing operational efficiency. The feasibility of these plans remains uncertain and is influenced by factors outside the company's control.

The company is under pressure to generate positive cash flow due to existing contractual obligations and anticipates ongoing investments in vehicle production, sales network expansion, and research and development (R&D). However, these investments may not result in timely revenue increases or positive cash flow. If the company fails to continue as a going concern, it may encounter difficulties in securing financing on acceptable terms, which raises substantial doubt about its operational viability. Furthermore, the financial statements do not account for potential adjustments should the company be unable to maintain normal operations, potentially leading to significant losses for investors. The company also faces risks such as lack of demand, increasing competition, and unforeseen expenses, all of which may impede its ability to generate revenue and achieve profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The automotive market is highly competitive, and we may not be successful in competing in this industry. 
summary: The global automotive market is highly competitive, characterized by significant barriers to entry such as high capital requirements, long vehicle development lead times, specialized expertise, regulatory compliance, and the establishment of brand identity and sales networks. The company has strategically positioned itself in the sustainable luxury Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) sector, facing unique challenges including the high costs of developing competitive electric powertrains, inexperience in servicing electric vehicles, regulatory issues concerning battery transport, and the necessity for adequate charging infrastructure. The competitive landscape is expected to intensify with new entrants, not only from traditional Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) vehicles but also from other new energy vehicles.

The company faces formidable competition, particularly from international players with greater financial, technological, and manufacturing resources, which may impede its ability to compete effectively. Key competitive factors include local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality, technological innovation, pricing, and customer service. Increased competition could result in reduced vehicle sales and higher inventory levels, adversely affecting the company's financial health and market share.

The company's reliance on partnerships, particularly with Geely Holding for research, development, manufacturing, and engineering, introduces operational risks and challenges related to independence and production targets. Critical agreements, such as the Technology License Agreement and Manufacturing Cooperation Agreement with Geely, are vital for leveraging Geely's expertise while focusing on differentiation in design and technology. However, this reliance also exposes the company to potential disputes and negative publicity that could impact its reputation and operational capabilities.

Founded in 2018, the company launched its first vehicle, the Eletre, in 2022, resulting in a limited operating history that complicates revenue predictions and the timely delivery of high-quality vehicles. The company's sustainability is contingent upon effectively executing its development and manufacturing plans, particularly at its Wuhan facility, which is essential for producing new BEV models. Challenges include potential delays in technology development, funding shortages, supply chain disruptions, quality control issues, and compliance with regulations.

Financially, the company has not achieved profitability, reporting net losses of $750.3 million in 2023, $724.6 million in 2022, and $110.5 million in 2021. Negative cash flows from operations were $386.9 million in 2023, $351.4 million in 2022, and $126.5 million in 2021. The accumulated deficit as of December 31 for each year stood at $1.6 billion in 2023, $846.8 million in 2022, and $122.8 million in 2021. Capital expenditures were $213.7 million in 2023, $133.3 million in 2022, and $34.6 million in 2021. The company has primarily financed its operations through the issuance of exchangeable and convertible notes and related party borrowings. 

To sustain operations over the next 12 months, the company requires additional liquidity and is exploring various funding strategies, including bank loans, related party loans, and operational improvements. However, the feasibility of these strategies is uncertain and may be affected by external factors. Future financing may be necessary to meet capital requirements or address unforeseen challenges, but there is no guarantee that financing will be available on acceptable terms. The company faces pressure to generate positive cash flow due to contractual obligations, including capital commitments and debts. Investments in production, sales, and R&D may not yield timely revenue increases or positive cash flow, raising substantial doubts about the company's ability to continue as a going concern. The financial statements do not reflect potential adjustments if the company cannot operate normally, which could lead to significant investor losses. Overall, the company must navigate these competitive and operational challenges while maintaining strong partnerships and brand integrity to secure its future in the automotive market.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We may not succeed in continuing to maintain and strengthen our brand, and our brand and reputation could be harmed by negative publicity with respect to us, our directors, officers, employees, shareholders, peers, business partners, or our industry in general. 
summary: The success of our business is intricately linked to the strength and perception of the "Lotus" brand, known for its design, sustainability, and technological innovation. We hold royalty-free licenses from Group Lotus Limited to use the "Lotus" trademarks for our lifestyle vehicles and related components. However, achieving a reputation akin to Lotus' sports vehicles is uncertain, and failure to do so could impede our customer growth. Our brand's success hinges on delivering high-quality vehicles and services, effective customer engagement, and robust marketing strategies, areas where we currently lack extensive experience.

The licenses we possess allow for exclusive use in specific contexts, but they are not without limitations, as third parties may also utilize the "Lotus" trademarks. The risk of termination of these licenses due to breaches or corporate changes poses a significant threat to our competitive position. Additionally, our reliance on Group Lotus Limited for trademark protection raises concerns about their commitment to enforcing these rights, which could adversely affect our business prospects.

Our operational history is limited, and while we have partnered with Geely Holding to manufacture battery electric vehicles (BEVs) at a facility in Wuhan, our ability to meet production targets and deliver high-quality automobiles remains unproven. The EV sector faces numerous challenges, including technological delays, supply chain disruptions, and compliance issues, which complicate our growth and operational efficiency.

Moreover, our dependence on third-party suppliers for critical components introduces additional risks, as any disruptions could delay vehicle deliveries and damage our reputation. Financially, the company has not achieved profitability since its inception, accumulating significant losses and negative cash flows, which raises doubts about our financial viability and ability to sustain operations without securing additional funding.

To navigate these challenges, we are exploring various funding strategies, including loans and potential equity financing. However, the success of these efforts is uncertain and contingent on external factors. The pressure to generate positive cash flow is compounded by contractual obligations and the need for substantial investments in production and development, which may not yield immediate returns. Overall, the company faces significant risks that could hinder revenue generation and lead to substantial losses, necessitating careful management and strategic planning to ensure long-term sustainability.

title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.
